data.title: Lululemon CEO joins the ranks of departing retail executives after tumultuous year at the athleisure company
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

Lululemon's stock rallies after CEO news, quarterly earnings

A Lululemon store in New York City. The company showed worrying sales trends in its Americas business, but strength elsewhere.

Investors cheered the departure of Lululemon Athletica's chief executive, with the athleisure-apparel maker's stock rallying more than 10% in the after-hours session Thursday.

Lululemon (LULU) said Thursday that CEO Calvin McDonald plans to step down on Jan. 31. McDonald and the board are working on a "smooth" transition, with the executive serving as a senior adviser to the company through March 31.

Chief Financial Officer Meghan Frank and Chief Commercial Officer André Maestrini will serve as interim co-CEOs, the company said.

The move caps an intense year at Lululemon, which saw its stock lose half of its value last year after a series of missteps. It also comes after some notable CEO changes at other retailers, including Walmart (WMT), which announced last month it will be getting a new CEO early next year.

Lululemon recently came under criticism from analysts for its partnership with the NFL, on concerns that it would further muddle the waters for a company better known for its yoga pants than for football merchandise. It also faces increased competition from the likes of privately held Vuori and Alo Yoga.

And Wall Street long has fretted that the athleisure category as a whole is on the losing side of a return to casual dressing and less momentum for loungewear and other of Lululemon's specialties.

Lululemon reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings late Thursday as well, which also boosted the stock even as the report showed weakness in the company's Americas business.

Lululemon's third-quarter sales rose 7% to $2.6 billion, including a drop of 2% for its Americas revenue and a 33% rise in its international revenue. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for sales of $2.5 billion in the quarter.

"As we enter the holiday season, we are encouraged by our early performance," McDonald said.

Same-store sales rose 1%, meeting FactSet consensus. It included a 5% drop in same-store sales in the Americas region and an 18% increase in same-store sales in international markets, the company said.

The company reported earnings of $2.59 a share, compared with $2.87 and FactSet consensus for $2.21 a share.

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Nantucket, Vineyard Wind finalize deal for implementing robust safety measures
data.description: (Platts)--11Dec2025/524 pm EST/2224 GMT **Vineyard Wind finalizes 14-point safety plan with Nantucket **Deal allows town to sue for breaches, no escrow account Nantucket, Massachusetts, and Vineyard Wind have finalized a deal to implement a 14-point response plan for limiting the impacts of a turbine blade collapse, similar to the one that occurred in 2024. Nantucket attorneys announced the Dec. 11 final deal, which brought an end to six months of negotiations. The attorneys said the deal does not preclude the town's government from suing the wind project developer if it fails to meet any of the 14 protocols. "This agreement is not symbolic — it is operational," Greg Werkheiser, legal counsel for Nantucket, said in a news release. "These are real-world measures that will meaningfully improve the community's ability to protect the Island." The final deal does not include the creation of a $10 million escrow account that the town had originally called for in early negotiations. Instead, the company is required to reimburse the town within 60 days for all "reasonable emergency response expenses." The agreement also does not include earlier town recommendations for enacting bans on new projects if future incidents prompt prolonged beach closures or shellfish harvesting bans. Not included was the possibility of a state enforcement mechanism if the company fails to meet its obligations. A preliminary deal negotiated in August said Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey had initiated talks with the town to identify potential enforcement mechanisms if Vineyard does not uphold any part of the agreement. In the aftermath of the July 2024 turbine failure, Nantucket criticized the company for insufficient updates and coordination. Hundreds of pounds of fiberglass and foam debris washed onto Nantucket beaches during the summer tourist season. The agreement calls for the company to provide detailed reports on the ongoing development of the project, timely responses to questions, sharing of regulatory documents, and prompt emergency notifications. In addition, the town will now be represented at emergency response meetings with the US Coast Guard and the Interior Department's Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement. A town representative would also be part of incident management and communications coordination teams. The company is required to share all critical findings from project reports within 10 days before they are finalized. The company is limited on what it can redact from those reports. RADAR SYSTEM Vineyard Wind agreed to provide detailed data on installing a radar-based, aircraft-detection lighting system that would light the turbines only when an aircraft is approaching. The town has complained about the visual effects of the dozens of lights used to illuminate the turbines at night. The company is now required to report periods when the radar lighting system is inoperable and if lighting exceeds original estimates provided to the US Bureau of Ocean Energy Management. The company is also required to determine the extent of the outage, identify the cause and explore potential solutions. BREACH OF CONTRACT If the company allegedly breaches any of the commitments, the parties would first engage in structured dispute resolution, "including mandatory meetings between top leadership and formal mediation," according to a summary of the deal. "The town does not forfeit its right to sue for damages arising from any breach of these terms," the summary said. Vineyard Wind is the only commercial offshore wind farm to begin transmitting power to the grid from a limited number of turbines. It is continuing construction of the 62-turbine, 806-MW project. The project was delayed after the wind blade implosion, which was caused by a manufacturing defect. The Trump administration often cites the incident to justify its decision to abandon the government's offshore wind licensing program. Nevertheless, the project has managed to keep its federal operating license and is nearing completion. — John Siciliano, john.siciliano@spglobal.com-- Edited by Lyle Crouse, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Adobe Brings Photoshop, Express And Acrobat Tools Into ChatGPT
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Adobe is ramping up its AI strategy by integrating key features from Photoshop, Express, and Acrobat right into ChatGPT.

This means users can now ask the chatbot to handle tasks like editing images, applying effects, modifying PDFs, or even animating design elements, all using Adobe's tools behind the scenes.

With Photoshop, ChatGPT can make adjustments to specific areas of images, like blurring or removing backgrounds, changing brightness and contrast, or applying effects using sliders.

The Express integration allows users to access existing designs, whip up themed visuals, tweak layouts, or animate different elements. Plus, with Acrobat's support, users can merge files, edit text, or pull tables from PDFs.

If users want to dive deeper into features or prefer to do edits themselves, they can easily switch over to Adobe's full apps at any time.

Adobe mentions that these new features are being rolled out globally on ChatGPT for desktop, web, and iOS, while currently on Android, only Express is available, with Photoshop and Acrobat coming soon.

This update comes after a year filled with significant AI releases from Adobe, including new assistants for Photoshop and Express and the preview of their cross-app AI bot called Project Moonlight.

As more third-party apps integrate with ChatGPT, there's increasing competition for users' attention within the chatbot, with Adobe now joining the ranks of Canva, Spotify, Expedia, and Figma.

Copyright(c) 2025 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Firefly Aerospace Names Ramon Sanchez as Operating Chief
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Firefly Aerospace named Ramon Sanchez as its chief operating officer.

Sanchez is scheduled to start on Dec. 22 and manage production scaling and operational execution, the defense technology company said Thursday.

Sanchez, 56, has worked at Boeing for 25 years.

He has experience with commercial, classified and human space flight missions, as well as with production operations for commercial, civil and national security programs.

Firefly plans to pay Sanchez an annual base salary of $425,000, plus a $42,000 sign-on bonus and a $50,000 relocation allowance.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Lululemon Says Black Friday Traffic Helped Cleanup Inventory — Market Talk
data.description: Lululemon saw higher traffic over the Black Friday sales period, helping it clear through some seasonal and old products, Chief Executive Calvin McDonald says during a call with analysts. That "helps position us well from an inventory standpoint as we exit quarter four and enter spring," McDonald says. Lululemon offered its Black Friday sale to its members a week earlier than last year, driving increased traffic to its e-commerce sites as well as more app downloads and signups for its membership program, he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Skillz Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Skillz Inc., a leading eSports gaming and monetization platform, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance, operational challenges, and strategic initiatives aimed at navigating a complex business environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $21.9 million, reflecting a decrease from $25.2 million in the prior year period, primarily due to reduced user acquisition and engagement marketing expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $(15.5) million, an improvement from $(26.9) million in the prior year period, driven by a gain from litigation settlement and reduced general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(17.1) million, compared to $(26.7) million in the prior year period, indicating a narrowing of losses due to cost management and a litigation settlement gain.
  • Loss per share, basic and diluted: $(1.05), compared to $(1.45) in the prior year period, reflecting the impact of reduced net loss and a decrease in weighted average shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Skillz operates two main segments: the Skillz segment, which focuses on eSports gaming and monetization services for game developers, and the Aarki segment, which provides advertising solutions using AI technology. For the three months ended March 31, 2025, the Skillz segment generated $17.6 million in revenue, while the Aarki segment contributed $4.4 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The majority of the company's revenue is generated in the United States, with $16.3 million from the Skillz segment and $1.3 million from the Aarki segment. Other countries contributed $1.3 million and $3.1 million, respectively, indicating a strong presence in international markets.
  • User Acquisition and Engagement: The company has reduced its user acquisition marketing spend to $4.5 million in Q1 2025 from $5.6 million in Q1 2024. This reduction, along with decreased engagement marketing expenses, has led to a substantial reduction in revenue, a trend expected to continue.
  • Litigation Impact: The company is involved in significant litigation with Tether, which accounted for 45% of its revenue in 2024. The outcome of this litigation could materially affect the company's business and operations.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on resolving its litigation with Tether and maintaining its listing on the NYSE by complying with filing requirements. The company is also working on regaining compliance with its reporting covenants under the Indenture for its 2021 Senior Secured Notes.
  • Operational Challenges: The company is facing challenges related to the termination of agreements by Tether, which could impact its platform if key games are removed. The company is actively seeking to resolve these issues through legal action.
  • Segment Adjusted EBITDA: The Skillz segment reported an adjusted EBITDA of negative $16.3 million, while the Aarki segment reported negative $973,000, reflecting operational challenges and ongoing investments in growth and litigation.

SEC Filing: Skillz Inc. [ SKLZ ] - 10-Q - Dec. 11, 2025

data.title: Odyssey Health, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Odyssey Health, Inc., a company dedicated to developing innovative medical technologies, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational progress, reflecting significant strides in reducing losses and advancing key product developments.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss and Comprehensive Loss: Odyssey Health reported a net loss and comprehensive loss of $(483,447), marking a 52.6% decrease compared to the prior year period. This improvement is primarily attributed to reduced general and administrative expenses.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: Both basic and diluted net loss per share were $(0.00), compared to $(0.01) in the prior year period, indicating a reduction in net loss per share.

Loss from Operations: The company reported a loss from operations of $(303,190), a 47.7% decrease from the prior year period. This reduction is mainly due to lower public company expenses and executive salary reductions.

Interest Expense: Interest expense increased by 38.6% to $(95,336), driven by higher weighted average debt outstanding.

Business Highlights

Product Development: Odyssey Health is focused on developing two key technologies: the CardioMap heart monitoring and screening device, and the Save-A-Life choking rescue device. Both products are currently in the research and development stage and have not yet received regulatory clearance or approval for commercial sale.

Business Model: The company aims to develop or acquire unique medical-related products, engage third parties for development and manufacturing, and distribute these products through various channels. This includes licensing and improving products, identifying distribution channels, and establishing agreements with distributors.

Future Outlook: Odyssey Health plans to continue its efforts in acquiring and developing medical technologies that address unmet medical needs. The company intends to engage third-party research and development firms to assist in product development and will apply for trademarks and patents as products advance.

Operational Challenges: The company is not currently selling or marketing any products, as they are still in development and require FDA clearance or approval to be marketed in the United States. Additionally, European Union or country-specific approvals would be needed for international sales.

Strategic Partnerships: On October 14, 2025, Odyssey Health entered into a Master Technology and Sub-license Agreement with NeuRX Health, Inc. for exclusive, worldwide rights to BreastCheck®, a non-invasive test for breast abnormalities. This agreement is expected to close in January 2026, subject to finalization of terms and conditions.

Going Concern: The company has expressed substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to its operating deficit, negative working capital, and lack of sufficient working capital to meet operating expenses through the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2026. The company is actively seeking additional capital to meet its obligations and execute its business plan.

SEC Filing: Odyssey Health, Inc. [ ODYY ] - 10-Q - Dec. 11, 2025

data.title: Netskope Inc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Netskope Inc, a leading provider of cloud security solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational developments, showcasing the company's robust revenue growth, strategic investments in research and development, and expansion efforts across various regions. Below is a detailed summary of the key financial and business highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $184.2 million, increased by 33% compared to the same period last year, driven by new customer additions and expansion within existing customers.
  • Gross Profit: $106.6 million, representing a gross margin of 58%, decreased from 66% in the prior year due to increased stock-based compensation expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $(447.0) million, significantly impacted by increased operating expenses, particularly in research and development and general and administrative expenses due to stock-based compensation.
  • Net Loss: $(453.1) million, compared to $(70.7) million in the prior year, primarily due to increased operating expenses and stock-based compensation related to the IPO.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(1.85), compared to $(0.72) in the prior year, reflecting the increased net loss and higher weighted-average shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Subscription revenue accounted for approximately 99% of the company's total revenue during the reporting period, indicating a strong reliance on recurring revenue streams from its Netskope One platform.
  • Geographical Performance: The Americas region, including the United States, Canada, and Latin America, contributed significantly to revenue, with the United States alone generating $82.8 million during the three months ended October 31, 2025. The Asia Pacific and Japan region and the Europe, Middle East, and Africa region also showed strong performance, contributing $33.9 million and $45.7 million, respectively, during the same period.
  • Customer Segments: Revenue from indirect channels, including channel partners and managed service providers, was substantial, accounting for $175.2 million of the total revenue for the three months ended October 31, 2025, highlighting the importance of these partnerships in the company's sales strategy.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in sales and marketing to expand its market presence and drive customer acquisition and retention. Additionally, there is a focus on research and development to enhance product features and respond to new cyber threats, which is expected to support long-term growth.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is prioritizing the modernization of security and networking solutions to meet the demands of cloud and AI-driven enterprise environments. This includes leveraging advanced technologies for granular visibility and control over information flows.
  • Sales and Marketing Expansion: The company is expanding its sales force and channel partnerships, particularly in the Americas, to capitalize on growth opportunities and increase penetration in the U.S. federal government market following FedRAMP High Authorization.
  • Research and Development Focus: Continued investment in research and development is planned to drive innovation and strengthen the company's technology leadership, with an emphasis on enhancing the Netskope One platform.
  • Key Business Metric: The dollar-based net retention rate was 118% as of October 31, 2025, reflecting strong customer retention and expansion within the existing customer base, which is crucial for sustaining long-term revenue growth.

SEC Filing: Netskope Inc [ NTSK ] - 10-Q - Dec. 11, 2025

data.title: KESTRA MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, LTD. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kestra Medical Technologies, Ltd., a company specializing in cardiac recovery systems, has released its Form 10-Q report for the period ending October 31, 2025. The report highlights significant growth in both financial and operational metrics, driven by increased adoption of its flagship product, the ASSURE WCD, a wearable cardioverter defibrillator designed to protect patients at elevated risk of sudden cardiac arrest (SCA).

Financial Highlights

Revenue: Kestra Medical Technologies reported revenue of $22.6 million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, marking a 53% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, revenue was $41.9 million, also a 53% increase from the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by a 54% increase in the number of patients using the company's products.

Gross Profit: The company's gross profit for the three months ended October 31, 2025, was $11.4 million, a 96% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, gross profit reached $20.3 million, a 102% increase from the previous year. The improvement in gross profit was attributed to revenue growth and cost efficiencies.

Loss from Operations: Kestra reported a loss from operations of $(31.8) million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, a 66% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, the loss from operations was $(60.6) million, reflecting a 61% increase from the previous year. The increased losses were primarily due to higher operating expenses.

Net Loss and Comprehensive Loss: The net loss and comprehensive loss for the three months ended October 31, 2025, was $(32.8) million, a 59% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, the net loss and comprehensive loss was $(58.6) million, a 43% increase from the previous year.

Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: The net loss attributable to common shareholders, basic and diluted, was $(32.8) million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to $(23.7) million for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, the net loss attributable to common shareholders was $(58.6) million, compared to $(46.0) million for the same period in 2024.

Net Loss Per Share: The net loss per share attributable to common shareholders, basic and diluted, was $(0.64) for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to $(1.19) for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended October 31, 2025, the net loss per share was $(1.14), compared to $(2.31) for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Product Offering: Kestra Medical Technologies focuses on its Cardiac Recovery System platform, which integrates monitoring, therapeutic treatment, digital health, and patient support services. The cornerstone of this platform is the ASSURE WCD, a wearable cardioverter defibrillator designed to protect patients at elevated risk of sudden cardiac arrest (SCA).

Operational Focus: The company is heavily investing in building its sales and marketing organization to support the commercialization of its Cardiac Recovery System platform in the United States. This includes recruiting, training, and retaining a direct sales force and supporting commercial infrastructure.

Manufacturing Strategy: Kestra outsources the manufacturing of its ASSURE WCD and its components to third-party suppliers. These partners are recognized for their competency and have established quality systems that meet FDA requirements. The company believes these manufacturers have sufficient capacity to meet anticipated demand.

Revenue Segments: The company generates nearly all its revenue from leasing the ASSURE WCD to patients, with payments coming from third-party payors such as Medicare, Medicaid, and private insurers, as well as patient co-payments.

Sales Units: Revenue growth was primarily driven by a 54% increase in the number of patients using the company's products for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024.

Future Outlook: Kestra plans to continue investing in research and development to enhance its products and services, and to support applications for regulatory approvals. The company also intends to increase the size of its commercial team to further market its products in the United States.

Market Access and Reimbursement: The company is working with private payors to ensure positive coverage decisions for its products and is investing in revenue cycle management infrastructure to improve cash collection from payors.

Operational Efficiency: Kestra is focusing on supply chain efficiencies through higher volume purchases of components and manufacturing process improvements. The company expects these efforts to contribute to increased gross profit margins.

Seasonality Impact: The company notes that its billings and collections efforts tend to be lower in January and February due to the resetting of annual patient healthcare insurance plan deductibles.

SEC Filing: KESTRA MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, LTD. [ KMTS ] - 10-Q - Dec. 11, 2025

data.title: Recent leadership changes at global consumer goods companies
data.description:
Lululemon Athletica said its CEO Calvin McDonald will step down in January as the yogawear maker navigates a challenging consumer environment in the United States.

Coca-Cola and Altria also named new CEOs recently, as the consumer sector goes through a flurry of leadership changes against the backdrop of tariff pressures, an uncertain economy and a choppy consumer spending backdrop.

Here are some of the major CEO changes among global consumer goods companies in the past year.

Company

Date of Announcement

Details

Lululemon Athletica

December 11, 2025

Lululemon Athletica said its CEO Calvin McDonald will step down in January after about seven years at the helm.

The company named its finance chief Meghan Frank and chief commercial officer André Maestrini as co-interim CEOs while it searches for its new boss.

Altria

December 11, 2025

Altria announced that CEO Billy Gifford, who has led the tobacco giant since 2020, will retire, effective May 14, 2026.

The company announced Gifford will be succeeded by finance head Salvatore Mancuso.

Coca-Cola

December 10, 2025

Coca-Cola named COO Henrique Braun as its new CEO, effective March 31, 2026.

Braun succeeds James Quincey, who is stepping down after nine years at the helm.

Kohl's Corp

November 24, 2025

Kohl's Corp named retail veteran Michael Bender as its permanent CEO, after he served as the interim chief since May.

Bender replaced Ashley Buchanan, who was fired for a personal relationship with a vendor.

Walmart

November 14, 2025

The company said Doug McMillon, who has been heading the retail bellwether since 2014, will retire in January 2026.

John Furner, McMillon's successor, currently serves as CEO of Walmart U.S. and has held leadership roles at the company.

Nestle

September 1, 2025

Nestle dismissed its CEO, Laurent Freixe, a year after appointing him, following an investigation into an undisclosed romantic relationship with a direct subordinate that breached the company's code of conduct.

Freixe was replaced by Philipp Navratil, CEO of Nestle Nespresso, on September 1.

Target

August 20, 2025

The retailer named longtime company veteran Michael Fiddelke as its CEO, replacing retail industry bigwig Brian Cornell, effective February 1, 2026.

Procter & Gamble

July 28, 2025

Procter & Gamble said CEO Jon Moeller is stepping away from the role, to be succeeded by Chief Operating Officer Shailesh Jejurikar.

Diageo

July 16, 2025

The Johnnie Walker whisky and Guinness beer maker's CEO, Debra Crew, stepped down after two years in the job, with finance chief Nik Jhangiani taking over in the interim.

Kenvue

July 14, 2025

The Band-Aid and Tylenol maker fired its CEO Thibaut Mongon, laying what some investors expect will be the groundwork for an eventual sale of the entire company or pieces of it, and named director Kirk Perry as interim CEO.

Hindustan Unilever

July 10, 2025

Hindustan Unilever named Priya Nair as managing director and CEO, replacing Rohit Jawa well before the completion of his five-year term as the company's chief.

Hershey

July 8, 2025

Hershey named burger chain Wendy's chief Kirk Tanner as its CEO, effective August 18, replacing Michele Buck, who is set to retire.

Stanley Black & Decker

June 30, 2025

The power tools maker appointed operations chief Christopher Nelson as its next CEO and president, effective October 1, succeeding Donald Allan Jr., who is set to retire.

Unilever

February 25, 2025

The company ousted chief executive Hein Schumacher, replacing him with finance chief Fernando Fernandez.
